solutions.solution: We can get a correct answer from the information provided. From the two statements together we see that A is the tallest because A is taller than both B and C, while B or C may be the shortest. From the two statements together it can be seen that the mother is 30 years old. The exact age of Mona cannot be found from the two statements. Exactly where the capital is, cannot be established by the two statements. The exact value of the coins cannot be known unless the denominations of the five remaining coins are also not found. From the two statements together it is obvious that there are 10 kinds and 10 cows. Hence, the answer is 10 + 4 = 60 legs.
Our answer is obtainable from statement II alone but not from statement I alone.
Data are inadequate to come to a definite conclusion. I implies Anju < Sonu; II implies Rohit < Sonu. We can't compare Anju and Rohit with the help of this data.
If the father is 20 years older than his son, he will always remain 20 years older than him. Both the statements say the same thing.
Since A = A = A, we can get a conclusion 'All books are chocolates'. This automatically implies that Some books are chocolates.
The two statements are not sufficient to come to a definite conclusion. It is obvious that B is the son of A but we do not know if A is the mother or father of B.

We cannot find the speed of the train from the given data. To find an answer to this question we will also need to know the length of the bridge. When a train crosses a bridge it covers a distance which is equal to the sum of the length of the train and that of the bridge. Since the length of the bridge is not given, the information is incomplete.

From the two statements together, we can find that the answer to the question is 7 = 49 per second. We can safely assume that a pole has no length and, hence, in passing a pole a train only covers a distance which is equal to its own length.
From the two statements together we can immediately see that A is the grandfather of B. Hence the answer to the question can be found. [The answer is: No.]
From the two statements together we can immediately see that A is the father of B. Hence, the answer to the question can be found. [The answer is: Yes.]
